text: What should you do when the alarm bell on a self-contained breathing apparatus sounds?

options.text: Open the bypass valve on the regulator and immediately evacuate the contaminated area.
Move the tank selector lever to the full tank position and reset the alarm so you can evacuate the area when it sounds again.
Move the reserve lever to the 'reserve' position on the regulator and reset the alarm so you can evacuate the area when it sounds again.

explanation: The alarm on a self-contained breathing apparatus (SCBA) signifies the low-pressure warning, indicating the air reserve is nearing depletion. The only safe action is to immediately retreat to a safe, non-contaminated environment.
